The Artist’s path

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The Artist’s Path
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
Key Takeaways:
• 	A lot of artists use PLF for their businesses and there are many success stories
• 	When Hollywood releases a film they use the exact same launch formula. The
difference for artists is that you’re not using the P.S. Path - you’re not really defining
a problem and solving it. Instead, you want to tell the story of the work and
foreshadow it
• 	This is why you always want to be building the list so you can use it foreshadow the
work. Social media is important for most artists, if not as important as the email list
How to Implement the Artist’s Path
• 	Introduce the “artist’s hero’s journey”
o 	People love the behind-the-scenes insider view
o 	If you use this leading up to a launch it works great
• 	Revelation of Self
o 	As an artist, people assume you have a romantic story
o 	Release this story, especially in regards to your work
• 	Engage your Tribe
o 	It’s easier for artists - people feel an attachment to your work
o 	Make people feel connected and they will get behind it
• 	Not necessary to have three pieces of prelaunch content
o 	What matters more is the story and giving people insight into your work
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The Artist’s Path
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT:
Now I want to talk about the artist’s path through Product Launch
Formula.
I have to admit when I first came out with Product Launch Formula, I didn’t
expect a lot of artists to use it. Lo and behold, they bought the product, they used it,
and it worked for them. We’re talking about sculptors, novelists, recording artists,
and painters. It’s just absolutely awesome, because I love art, I have a real artistic
streak myself, and it’s just awesome that I started getting these success stories.
Really, it makes sense if you look at the way Hollywood does it when they
release a film, which is an artistic work, they totally sequence it. They have trailers
and they find all different ways to get it in popular culture, and they create buzz
around their launch. It absolutely works.
Product Launch Formula sequencing is awesome for art; whatever kind of
artist you are. But it’s not so much the problem-solution path. Those mental triggers
still work, the sequencing still works, but you’re not really defining a problem and
then solving that problem.
You are delivering a lot of value. If you want to think about their problem,
they need entertainment or they need enlightenment, and you’re giving them a
solution. Yes, sure. But it’s not a perfect fit into that problem-solution path.
You still want to tell a story throughout your Prelaunch Content. It’s really the
story of the creation of the artwork, whether it’s a novel or a painting, or whatever.
You want to foreshadow the work. That’s one of the easiest biggest things you want to
do. It’s like a movie trailer, it’s a massive foreshadowing of the work.
This is why you always want to be building your list, so that you can use that
list to foreshadow the work, whether it’s social media or email. I highly recommend
email, but social media for most artists is also an important adjunct to your email
list.
Remember that product launch hero’s journey? Here it’s sort of the artist’s
hero journey. You tell the story of the creation. If you think about when they come
out with “the making of the Titanic,” or “the making of” this or that, it’s always the
behind the scenes. People love to feel like they’re behind the scenes and they have an
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

4
TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The Artist’s Path
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
insider view. They love that. If you do that leading up into the launch, that is
absolutely awesome.
Then another piece is a little bit of revelation of yourself. If you’re an artist,
people instantly assume that you have a super romantic life, that there’s something
really cool going on. People love that. I know I’ve gotten to meet some famous
authors and just being able to rub shoulders with them is pretty amazing. You might
think, “I’m just sitting at my desk here typing away all day long,” or, “I’m just sitting
here in this cramped room painting and I have paint all over me, there’s nothing
romantic about that.” But your followers instantly assume that there is something
really cool. Give them little glimpses into you and what your life is like, especially
with relation to creating the work that you’re going to release.
Another thing you want to do is engage your tribe. This is something that is a
lot easier for artists to do than it is for me. I’m putting out a product about building
business, so people don’t instantly want to spread the word. Frankly, if you want to
spread the word about PLF, that’s awesome. Thank you and please do so.
Generally, if you’re an artist, people really feel an emotional attachment to
you and to your work, if you’re doing everything right, and they’re going to want to
help spread the word, especially if you give them that insider view and they got to feel
like they watched the creation. They really start to feel an ownership and they’re all
about getting behind it.
Social media is great for allowing them to spread the word. It’s not necessarily
going to be three pieces of Prelaunch Content, three videos and then release it. But
you tell the story and the story is the creation of your work. Start to foreshadow the
work, give them glimpses, previews, give them glimpses into the way you created it
and the way that you built it.
All those things, that’s the artist’s path. People love that. You will build up
your tribe and each release will get better and better as you build up your tribe.
The artist’s path is not so much about defining that big problem and then
solving it, it’s more pulling people in with the story of the creation of your work.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
